ads.author: Alex Arias
ads.title: kaabo mantis 10 lite
ads.description: The Kaabo Mantis 10 Lite is a nimble, dual-motor electric scooter designed for urban commuting. It features dual 500W motors, a top speed of 31 mph, up to 37 miles of range, and dual spring suspension, all while weighing a manageable 62 lbs. Smartwheel USA +2 Performance & Battery Top Speed: Up to 31 mph (50 km/h) Max Range: Up to 37 miles (under optimal conditions: flat terrain, a 165 lb rider, and 15 mph cruising speed) Motor: Dual 500W brushless motors (2200W max power output) Battery: 48V 13Ah Li-ion (UL-Certified) Charging Time: Approximately 6.5 hours (with standard 2A charger) Climbing Angle: Up to 25 degrees Kaabo USA Electric Scooter +2 Build & Dimensions Weight: 62 lbs (28 kg) Max Load Capacity: 265 lbs (120 kg) Tires: 10" x 2.5" pneumatic tube tires on split rims Suspension: Front and rear C-Type dual spring shock absorbers Frame Material: Aviation-grade 6082/6081-T6 aluminum alloy Folded Dimensions: 49.9" L x 22" W x 18.9" H Speedway Riders NYC +3 Safety & Features Braking System: Dual 140mm mechanical disc brakes + EABS (Electronic Anti-lock Braking System) with regenerative braking Lighting: Front LED light, deck running lights, and turn signals Display: Standard LT01 LCD display (located on the throttle)

ads.author: Jason Beaver
ads.title: Super Mario Bros Arcade Machine
ads.description: Original Nintendo Super Mario Bros. Arcade Machine – Working Up for sale is an original Super Mario Bros. arcade cabinet. Cabinet powers on and plays fine with no issues during gameplay. Great piece for collectors, game rooms, arcades, retro stores, or anyone wanting a classic Nintendo cabinet. Condition: • Fully working and playable • Original CRT monitor with good picture • Screen was professionally recapped within the past 3 years • Cabinet shows cosmetic wear from age/use • Missing Player 2 button (shown in photos) • General scratches/scuffs consistent with vintage arcade machines • Includes coin doors and original style controls This is a real vintage arcade machine — not a multicade conversion. Awesome display piece and a fun playable classic. Must be picked up in Winter Haven, FL. Machine is heavy, bring help/loading assistance for transport. Sold as-is due to age.

ads.author: Patrick DeCristo
ads.title: Fender Limited Edition Fender x PAC-MAN Player II Telecaster
ads.description: Fender Limited Edition Fender x PAC-MAN Player II Telecaster $1549 + shipping *No Trades Please* Fender’s initial release consisted of only 65 guitars worldwide, and they sold out within minutes of launch. This limited edition Fender x PAC-MAN Player II Telecaster is in mint condition and was only removed from the box for testing and photography. There are no cosmetic blemishes to note anywhere on the instrument. Includes the original Fender gig bag, factory paperwork, and will be shipped in the original Fender shipping box. Created in collaboration with Bandai Namco, this incredibly limited Fender x PAC-MAN release celebrates one of the most iconic video games of all time with a bold, collectible twist on the legendary Telecaster platform. Designed to bridge generations of players and collectors alike, this model combines unmistakable PAC-MAN nostalgia with the modern playability and performance of the Player II Series. Whether you’re a serious collector, lifelong gamer, or working musician looking for something truly unique, this Telecaster delivers both visual impact and professional-level functionality. The guitar features a comfortable Modern “C” neck profile, a 9.5” radius fingerboard with rolled edges, and 22 medium jumbo frets for smooth, effortless playability. The body showcases vibrant PAC-MAN arcade graphics on the front, while the classic PAC-MAN logo adorns the back, making this one of the most visually distinctive Fender collaborations ever produced. Equipped with Player Series Alnico V Single-Coil Telecaster pickups, this guitar produces the classic Tele tone players expect — crisp highs, punchy mids, and tight, articulate lows. A 3-way blade switch, 6-saddle bridge, and Fender ClassicGear tuners provide reliable tuning stability and versatile tonal control for both studio and stage use. Features include: Limited Edition Fender x PAC-MAN collaboration Extremely limited initial run of only 65 guitars Modern “C” maple neck 9.5” fingerboard radius with rolled edges 22 medium jumbo frets Player Series Alnico V Telecaster single-coil pickups 3-way blade switch 6-saddle Telecaster bridge Fender ClassicGear tuners PAC-MAN artwork on front and logo on back Original Fender gig bag and paperwork included A rare and highly collectible collaboration that perfectly blends arcade nostalgia with classic Fender craftsmanship.

ads.author: Kenneth Alan
ads.title: Psychic Force 2012 – Sega Dreamcast – Japan Import NTSC-J Taito 3D Fighting Game
ads.description: Original Japanese release of Psychic Force 2012 for the Dreamcast, developed and published by Taito. This fast-paced 3D fighting game features psychic-powered combat, aerial movement, and anime-inspired character designs, making it a standout title among late-1990s and early-2000s arcade fighters. Game Details Title: Psychic Force 2012 Platform: Sega Dreamcast Region: NTSC-J (Japan Import) Publisher / Developer: Taito Genre: 3D Fighting Game Language: Japanese Format: GD-ROM Features Psychic-powered combat gameplay Full 3D movement and arena battles Arcade-style anime fighting action Japanese Dreamcast import release

ads.author: Kenneth Alan
ads.title: StarBlade – Sega Mega-CD / Sega CD – Namco 1994 Japan NTSC-J – Rare Shooter Game
ads.description: Original Japanese release of Starblade for the Sega Mega-CD / Sega CD, released in 1994 by Namco. This classic sci-fi space shooter is known for its cinematic presentation, scaling effects, and arcade-style gameplay. Originally an impressive arcade title, StarBlade became one of the standout shooter experiences available on CD-based systems during the 1990s. A highly collectible release for Sega CD and retro shooter fans. Game Details Title: StarBlade Platform: Sega Mega-CD / Sega CD Region: NTSC-J (Japan Import) Year Released: 1994 Publisher / Developer: Namco Genre: Shooter / Space Combat / Arcade Language: Japanese Format: CD-ROM Features Classic sci-fi space shooter gameplay Arcade-style cinematic presentation Early polygon-style visual effects Rare Japanese Sega Mega-CD release

ads.author: David Jenkins
ads.title: Dealer's Choice Williams EM Pinball from 1973
ads.description: Classic Williams dealers choice, electromechanical pinball machine from 1973. Has been restored and plays beautifully! Pros: - All the rubbers have been replaced to keep the ball bouncing smoothly. - All the light bulbs have been replaced with most being #47 bulbs because they produce less heat so they’ll be less likely to damage plastic overtime. All the bulbs in the playfield are warm white leds that produce even less heat. To prevent damage to the printed plastic pieces on the playfield. - All the Mylar under the pop bumpers has been replaced as well as two of the skirts on the pop pumps. - Back glass has been sprayed with triple thick layers to prevent delamination and make it last for years to come. - All switches have been cleaned and adjusted. - Playfield has been stripped down, cleaned and waxed. - Has been fully play, tested and works as intended. - Is a tough but fun game with lots of interesting mechanics and ways to score. Feel free give it a try if you’re interested. - In a first floor garage for easy pick up. Cons: - It’s an electromechanical pinball machine so it’s a bit loud. - Outside paint is original and so it’s slightly flaking in some places. Typical for machines this old. - Playfield has some slight wear but nothing to noticeable. Some paint matching has been done on the playfield to reverse damage most notably around the left pop bump. - Backglass has some slight fading mainly on the red and there some flacking but nothing noticeable. - Will require future maintenance. Pinballs occasionally need to be waxed and clean to prevent the ball from rolling dust and debris around, causing damage to the paint on the playfield. It’s not hard to do but it is important to keep in mind. - Heavy, it’s full of metal switch and coils so it weighs good a bit but don’t worry I’ll help you load it. I normally restore arcade machines with this being my first pinball machine so I can’t guarantee it’s perfect but I’ll say it’s pretty darn good. I record all the work I do on these machines and post it to YouTube, so feel free to watch the footage for yourself and see what’s been done to restore this machine back to its former glory at “Jenxed Arcade” on YouTube Note not all the videos on this machine have been posted at the time of this listing so just ask and I can send you the links the videos otherwise you’ll have to wait for them to release. Price is firm

ads.title: *** Vintage Rare 1975 inAir Aces” Pinball Game*** Great Condition
ads.description: This 1975 rare aviation “Air Aces” pinball game. Just professionally over hauled. Many new parts (bumpers and bands) and some extra comes with it. ***Works Perfectly and looks super clean! Unit #2808 *** Has key for back panel and will have new front lock with key *** Comes with original schematic sheets, couple of extra balls, replacment bulbs and fuses The item shown is a Bally Air Aces pinball machine, originally released in 1975. Key features of this vintage electro-mechanical (EM) pinball machine include: Manufacturer: Bally Manufacturing Corporation. Release Year: 1975. Player Capacity: Designed for up to 4 players. Gameplay Mechanics: It features a unique layout with two sets of flippers and a horizontal bank of nine drop targets at the top of the playfield. Production: Approximately 3,085 units were produced. *** Great for any aviation collector, Gift for pilot, mancave or have lots of fun with the family! (4 players) Check out my other listings! Located in North Port in

ads.author: Clifton Pennington
ads.title: Vintage Penny game. It is an ABT Challenger from 1925!
ads.description: Experience the thrill of vintage arcade excitement with the 1925 A.B.T. mfg co Challenger 1 Cent Penny Arcade Target Shooting Game. A true collector's piece, this original coin-op shooting game hails from the United States, where it was meticulously crafted to provide hours of entertainment. Its timeless design and historical value make it a standout addition to any collection. Delve into the golden age of arcade gaming with this classic non-video arcade machine. The year 1925 marks a period when arcade games were the epitome of social entertainment, and this piece embodies that era. Whether you're a seasoned collector or new to the world of vintage arcade games, the Challenger offers a glimpse into a bygone period of fun and skill. Others selling for over 1k.

ads.author: Scotchie Dorward
ads.title: Pinball Williams Jungle Lord beautifully restored
ads.description: I am looking to sell first but if you have a project Bally/early Stern or Williams I would trade +/- cash. This is a full size commercial pinball machine. NOT a toy - NOT a 1up arcade and NOT a virtual game. The real deal! Williams Jungle Lord game has been restored beautifully. All new cabinet decals and cabinet trim painted. Beautiful original playfield with very minor wear and original backglass in good condition. Just plug it in and play! Game plays perfectly - 100%. Every switch, solenoid, light and display works as it should. All new LED bulbs throughout the game. Top and bottom of playfield all cleaned and inspected. Legs painted and new leg levelers. New drop target decals and rubbers. The mpu/driver board has been replaced with a Rottendog MPU327. Works perfectly as it should. All other boards have been tested and perform perfectly as they should. 1 of the coin slots works with quarters or I have it set for free play now. Your choice - easy to add or take off free play. You can pick up in Port St Lucie, FL. 34986. I can also deliver and set up 1st floor only within 75 miles of 34986 for $150. Please message me if you need delivery outside of this area so we can discuss.

ads.title: Donkey Kong Junior by Nintendo - Original Dedicated Arcade Machine Cabinet
ads.description: I’ve owned the cabinet almost 10 years, adult played only, and has seen very little use. I bought the cabinet from a collector in St. Joseph, Missouri. He stated he owned the cabinet for 15 years. Everything on the cabinet is functional and operates as expected. High score save kit is installed and set to free play. One of the coin mechs are missing. I’m not aware of a single issue. Everything on the cabinet is original.
